missing_data: Gardens by Mirei Shigemori, Ogawa Jihei VII (lesser-known despite fame), Kobori Enshu locations, Kyoto private gardens.
improvement_hint: Index garden-master names (作庭家) as facetted attribute; boost spots that mention designer name.
_query: Traditional Japanese gardens designed by lesser-known garden masters.

reasoning: category=craft returns 231 METI items in id order — Nibutani Attus, Oitama Tsumugi, Ugo Shina. Some are dyeing-relevant (Oitama uses pre-dye 先染め, kusakizome) but textile dyeing-specific crafts (京友禅, 加賀友禅, 江戸小紋, 久留米絣, 有松絞り) are not at the top.
missing_data: Kyo-Yuzen, Kaga-Yuzen, Edo Komon, Arimatsu Shibori, Bingata, Kurume-gasuri — major dyeing crafts.
improvement_hint: Add industry sub-category 'dyeing 染色' to filter category=craft beyond the broad textile bucket.
_query: Where can I experience Japan's traditional textile dyeing techniques?

reasoning: search_area q=宿場 returns 91. Top hit Yanagimachi Ueda (Hokkoku Kaido post town) is excellent; second Suhara/Nojiri Nakasendo is excellent; then noise (Hiroshima boilerplate, STAY YAMAGATA, Iwate cookie banner). Real Tsumago/Magome/Narai not in the visible top.
missing_data: Tsumago-juku, Magome-juku, Narai-juku, Ouchi-juku, Sekijuku — most famous preserved post-towns.
improvement_hint: De-rank cookie-policy and prefecture-portal pages; boost spots that explicitly mention 宿場町 in name.
_query: Areas where you can still see remnants of Edo-period highway culture.

reasoning: Query asks for pilgrimages other than Shikoku 88. Default get_japan_heritage returns story 001 (Educational Heritage) first — irrelevant. Pilgrimage-related stories (西国33, 坂東33, 熊野古道, 出羽三山) are not surfaced. Constraint 'beyond Shikoku 88' is ignored.
